威爾士布雷康史爾利安福連亞賀
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Aubrey於1570年1月12日出生在威爾士布雷康史爾利安福連亞賀時，她的父親Rev. William AUBREY23歲，母親Lady Wilgiford Millicent WILLIAMS38歲。她於1590年與John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach在英國爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1654年12月22日在英國米德萊可易阿林聖 馬也斯享年84歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可哈洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Aubrey于1570年1月12日出生在威尔士布雷康史爾利安福連亞賀时，她的父亲Rev. William AUBREY23岁，母亲Lady Wilgiford Millicent WILLIAMS38岁。她于1590年与John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach在英国爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1654年12月22日在英国米德萊可易阿林聖 馬也斯享年84岁，埋葬在英国英格兰米德萊可哈洛。

nameMeanings.name: Aubrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English male personal name Albry, Aubry, or Aubery, a borrowing of Old French Alberi, Aubri (from ancient Germanic Alberic, which is composed of the elements alb ‘elf’ and ric ‘powerful’).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Albrey, Aubrey, a borrowing from Old French Albree, Aubree, Auberee (from ancient Germanic Albrad(a)).

English: variant of Abery, a habitational name from a local pronunciation of Avebury, Wiltshire. The placename derives from Old English burh ‘stronghold, fortified place’, with an uncertain first element, possibly the Old English personal name Afa.

variants.name: Albury
meanings: habitational name from one or other of various places called Albury in Hertfordshire, Oxfordshire, and Surrey, from Old English (e)ald ‘old’ + byrig, dative of burh ‘stronghold’.

variants.name: Aubry
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Michel, Pierre, Emile, Gaston, Germaine, Jacques, Jean Claude, Jean Michel, Jean-Michel, Luce, Stephane.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Alberic composed of the elements alb ‘elf’ and ric ‘powerful’. Compare Aubrey and Obrey .
French Canadian: altered form of Irish O'Brennan, an Anglicized form of Ó Braonáin (see Brennan 2).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Aubrey was born on 12 January 1570, in Llanfrynach, Breconshire, Wales, her father, Rev. William AUBREY, was 23 and her mother, Lady Wilgiford Millicent WILLIAMS, was 38. She married John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach in 1590, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 22 December 1654, in St Mary's, Ealing, Middlesex, England, at the age of 84, and was buried in Harrow, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Aubrey est née le 12 janvier 1570, à Llanfrynach, Breconshire, Galles, son père, Rev. William AUBREY, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Lady Wilgiford Millicent WILLIAMS, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach en 1590, à Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée le 22 décembre 1654, à St Mary's, Ealing, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterrée à Harrow, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Dezember 1654 im Alter von 84 Jahren in St Mary's, Ealing, Middlesex, England und wurde in Harrow, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Aubreyが1570年1月12日にウェールズブレコンシュアイアランフリナチで生まれた時，父Rev. William AUBREYは23歳，母Lady Wilgiford Millicent WILLIAMSは38歳であった。1590年にイギリスチェシュアイアでJohn Maddox Lord of Llanfrynachと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1654年12月22日にイギリスミッドレセックスエアリングスト マリ'スで84歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスハーローにて埋葬された。

ko.Jamestown, James City, Virginia, United: 미국 버지니아 주 자메스 시티 자메스타운
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Aubrey(이)가 1570년 1월 12일 웨일스 브레콘실르 랜프리나치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rev. William AUBREY(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Wilgiford Millicent WILLIAMS(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1590년 영국 체실르에서 John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1654년 12월 22일 영국 미들레세스 올잉 스트 마이'스에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 해로에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Elizabeth Aubrey nasceu em 12 de janeiro de 1570, em Llanfrynach, Breconshire, Gales, seu pai, Rev. William AUBREY, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Lady Wilgiford Millicent WILLIAMS, tinha 38 anos. Ela casou-se com John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach em 1590, em Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 22 de dezembro de 1654, em St Mary's, Ealing, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 84 anos, e foi sepultada em Harrow, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Aubrey родилась 12 января 1570 г. в местности лланфринач, брекуншир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Rev. William AUBREY было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Wilgiford Millicent WILLIAMS, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Maddox Lord of Llanfrynach в 1590 г. в местности чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла 22 декабря 1654 г. в местности ст мэри'с, эалинг, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности харроу, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.

Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 22 de diciembre de 1654, en St Mary's, Ealing, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultada en Harrow, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
